evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: developing robust and efficient data pipelines and data models
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: The word 'robust' paired with pipeline/model development signals a quality- and durability-oriented framing, matching rigour.
evidence.domain_risk: Make our data ready to use by our end-users
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: The data supports internal end-user consumption and decisions but carries no regulatory or financial-reporting stake, matching moderate risk.
evidence.collaboration_width: Data Engineers (DE); Data Analysts (DA); Data Scientists (DS)
evidence.data_team_maturity: building up our data assets and consolidating existing ones
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Explicitly 'building up' data assets while also consolidating existing ones reflects a stack in active growth rather than a from-scratch first hire or a mature governance-heavy function, matching mid maturity.
evidence.jd_authorship: Interact with your data peers to advocate good practices among the practitioners
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: The description is generic language about advocating good practices without specific tools, scale, or methodology detail, matching recruiter authorship.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: It's pivotal in the definition of setting up standards and guidelines for other data practitioners as well as applying the first levels of governance
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role's focus on standards and governance for other data practitioners centers it on serving internal data consumers, matching internal_data orientation.
evidence.autonomy_level: participating at setting up the architecture direction
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The word 'participating' signals contributing to direction-setting alongside others rather than solely owning it or solely executing scoped tasks, matching mixed autonomy.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Build-up our Data Warehouse by developing robust and efficient data pipelines and data models
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The JD combines building up new warehouse infrastructure with consolidating existing data assets, mixing new-build and fix/consolidate work, matching mixed.
evidence.ai_role: developing robust and efficient data pipelines and data models
evidence.ai_role_explanation: All phrases describe standard data engineering work with no mention of AI coding tools, GenAI infrastructure, or AI-assisted development.
evidence.testing_framing: It's pivotal in the definition of setting up standards and guidelines for other data practitioners as well as applying the first levels of governance
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Standards and governance mentioned but no explicit testing responsibility verb or testing tools listed.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: developing robust and efficient data pipelines; applying the first levels of governance
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Emphasis on 'robust' pipelines and operational governance indicates reliability concerns, though without explicit incident/SLO/stability language.
